raw_consumer_statement: Citi reversed a conditional credit on my XXXX credit card bill, which is due XX/XX/year>, indicating that I am accountable for the {$350.00} charge from XX/XX/year>. On XX/XX/year>, I contacted and spoke with a representative, explaining that a vendor was making unauthorized payments to my card. They closed my account and issued me a new card on XX/XX/XXXX, but the vendor was able to continue charging my card since Citi failed to turn off the digital element of the card. They apologized and said the system generally closes the card 's digital part, but something went wrong. They apologized and stated that they would reach out to the vendor for the unautorized charge. Since then, I've gone back and forth with Citi. They insist there is nothing they can do. My issue is that I contacted them as soon as I learned the company was billing my card unlawfully. I'm just not sure why I'm accountable. If Citi had shut down the digital side when they closed my account on XX/XX/XXXX, the {$350.00} card on XX/XX/XXXX would not have been processed. I've never received any items from the company, and their website is no longer operational.
